member.name: Blake Stephenson
member.synopsis: Blake Stephenson is the Conservative MP for Mid Bedfordshire , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Project Gigabit: Mid Bedfordshire
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, if she will ensure that the 5,443 properties descoped from Project Gigabit in Mid Bedfordshire constituency in May 2026 are added to a future programme.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As a result of the recent changes agreed by Building Digital UK (BDUK) and CityFibre, the earlier stages of the Bedfordshire, Northamptonshire and Milton Keynes contract will proceed as planned, and premises in the later stages will be removed from the co...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Immigration
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, if she will make it her policy to adopt Recommendation 14 of the report Backdoors to Britain, published by the hon. Member for Mid Bedfordshire on 4 March 2026.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government keeps the immigration system under review.

writtenQuestions.heading: Renewable Energy: Landfill
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what his planned timetable is for taking a decision on transitional arrangements for landfill gas operation before the closure of the Renewables Obligation in April 2027.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Government is aware that the commercial viability of some landfill gas sites may be affected when the Renewables Obligation begins to expire from 2027. DESNZ will shortly consult on proposals and an anticipated delivery timetable for a possible transition...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Renewable Energy: Landfill
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what assessment has been made of the relative cost to the public purse of (a) providing transitional support for landfill gas generation and (b) responsible parties bearing the unsupported co
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Landfill Directive (1999/31/EC) requires landfill operators to manage gas emissions to minimise risks to human health and the environment, including through flaring or, to the extent possible, by generating electricity from captured methane. The Renew...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Aviation: Fuels
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what recent steps her Department has taken to increase the supply of (a) sustainable aviation fuel and( b) domestic aviation fuel production.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Low Carbon Fuels Fund (LCFF) will support UK sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) supply by funding first-of-a-kind SAF production plants through the project pipeline to aid their commercialisation. Up to £219m has been made available through to 2029/30, w...

writtenQuestions.heading: Local Government Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of her policies for fiscal devolution in relation to income tax on trends in the level of funding for local authorities.
writtenQuestions.answerText: At her Mais Lecture, the Chancellor committed to work with mayors, businesses and other stakeholders to develop a fiscal devolution roadmap, to be published at the Budget, setting out plans to give regional leaders control over the allocation of a share o...

writtenQuestions.answerText: Every Child Can will invest £132.5 million of dormant assets funding to increase disadvantaged young people’s access to enrichment opportunities exclusively across five key categories of enrichment: civic engagement; arts and culture; nature, outdoor and ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

Name of donor: Energy and Climate Intelligence Unit Address of donor: 180 Borough High Street, London SE1 1LB Estimate of the probable value (or amount of any donation): Accommodation (£292.41), Flights (£2,271.72) and Maximum subsistence received (£835.64), value £3,339.77 Destination of visit: Brazil (Belém, Pará) Dates of visit: 11 November 2025 to 18 November 2025 Purpose of visit: Attendance of COP30 to meet legislators and experts and to build knowledge of the international climate process. Name of donor: Borneo Orangutan Survival (BOS) UK Address of donor: 483 Green Lanes, London N13 4BS Estimate of the probable value (or amount of any donation): Flights (£2,187.65), accommodation (£360.94), meals (£148.67), ground transportation (£124.00) and pre-trip medical tests (£340.00), value £3,161.26 Destination of visit: Indonesia (Jakarta and Borneo) Dates of visit: 26 March 2026 to 4 April 2026 Purpose of visit: Visit to meet legislators and experts and to build knowledge of the impacts of deforestation in Indonesia and the importance of conservation work to the natural world.
